chart.title: Time-based view of statistically abnormal failure spikes. Each bar represents detected instability events.

search.query: `svc_instability($index$)` | where unstable=1 | timechart span=15m count BY service

viz.title: Heatmap showing concentration of failure bursts across services and time. Darker colors indicate

table.title: Counts of detected recovery transitions where services returned to normal operation after failures.

chart.title: Comparison of failure events versus recovery events over time. A widening gap indicates worsening instability without sufficient recovery.

search.query: `svc_failure_detect($index$)` | sort service _time | streamstats current=f last(is_failure) AS prev_failure BY service | eval recovered = if(prev_failure=1 AND is_failure=0, 1, 0) | timechart span=30m sum(is_failure) AS failures sum(recovered) AS recoveries

div.p: Flapping refers to repeated short outages where a service fails and recovers frequently. These issues are often hidden in logs and may not trigger traditional alerts.

ul.li: Failure counts are compared against each service’s historical baseline.
Spikes beyond normal variation are flagged as instability.
Instability Timeline – Repeating spikes indicate flapping behavior.
Error Burst Heatmap – Darker regions represent concentrated failures.
Recovery Events – High recovery counts suggest restart loops or auto-healing.
Investigate recent deployments or configuration changes.
Check retry logic, circuit breakers, and dependency stability.
